services.name: API Gateway Service
services.responsibility: Entry point for all client requests, request routing, authentication/authorization enforcement, rate limiting, and potentially request transformation.

services.name: Auth Service
services.responsibility: Handles user registration, login, token issuance (e.g., JWT), password management, and user authentication.

services.name: User Profile Service
services.responsibility: Manages user profiles, shipping addresses, and other personal information, separate from authentication concerns.

services.name: Product Catalog Service
services.responsibility: Manages product information, categories, attributes, pricing, and integrates with search capabilities.

services.name: Shopping Cart Service
services.responsibility: Manages shopping cart items, quantities, and allows adding, removing, or updating products in a user's cart.

services.name: Order Service
services.responsibility: Handles order creation, manages order status transitions, provides order history, and orchestrates the checkout workflow.

services.name: Payment Service
services.responsibility: Processes payments, integrates with external payment gateways (e.g., Stripe, PayPal), handles payment authorization, capture, and refunds, ensuring PCI DSS compliance.

services.name: Shipping Service
services.responsibility: Manages shipping options, calculates shipping costs, integrates with external shipping carriers (e.g., FedEx, UPS), and tracks shipments.

services.name: Inventory Service
services.responsibility: Manages product stock levels, reserves inventory during order placement, and updates stock upon order fulfillment or cancellation.

services.name: Review Service
services.responsibility: Manages product reviews and ratings submitted by users.

services.name: Notification Service
services.responsibility: Sends transactional notifications (e.g., order confirmation, shipping updates) via email or SMS by integrating with external providers (e.g., SendGrid, Twilio).

services.name: Admin Service
services.responsibility: Provides APIs for administrators to manage products, orders, users, and other system configurations via an admin dashboard.

boundaries: API Gateway Service -> Microservices (Auth, Product Catalog, Shopping Cart, etc.)
Microservices -> External APIs (Payment Gateway, Shipping Carrier, Email/SMS Provider)
communication: REST/HTTP (Synchronous for client-to-gateway and most inter-service requests)
Kafka Event Streaming (Asynchronous for event-driven workflows like order processing, inventory updates, notifications, ensuring decoupling and fault tolerance)
External API Calls (HTTP/REST for integration with Payment Gateways, Shipping Carriers, and Notification Providers)
persistence: PostgreSQL: For core transactional data requiring strong ACID properties (e.g., User profiles, Orders, Inventory, Shipping addresses).
MongoDB: For flexible, semi-structured data (e.g., Product Catalog details, Product Reviews).
Redis: For high-speed caching (e.g., frequently accessed product data, user sessions), and volatile shopping cart data.
Elasticsearch: For full-text search capabilities on the product catalog and potentially for logs/metrics.
deployment: Kubernetes Cluster (for container orchestration, deployment, and management of all microservices)
Cloud Platform (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) for hosting Kubernetes and managed services
Managed Database Services (e.g., AWS RDS for PostgreSQL, AWS DocumentDB/MongoDB Atlas for MongoDB, AWS ElastiCache for Redis, AWS OpenSearch for Elasticsearch)
CDN (e.g., AWS CloudFront) for serving static assets (images, CSS, JS) to reduce latency and offload origin servers.
scalability: Horizontal auto-scaling for all stateless microservices using Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) based on CPU/memory utilization or custom metrics.
Database read replicas for PostgreSQL to offload read traffic and improve performance.
Database sharding/clustering for MongoDB, Elasticsearch, and Redis to distribute data and handle increased load.
Asynchronous processing with Kafka to decouple services, absorb traffic spikes, and enable high throughput for event-driven workflows.
Caching layer with Redis to reduce direct database load and improve response times for frequently accessed data.
CDN integration to distribute static content globally, reducing load on application servers and improving user experience.

diagram_versions.component diagram:
@startuml
!pragma layout smetana

title Microservices E-commerce System - Component Diagram

' Define external systems
rectangle "Client Applications" <<External System>> as ClientApp
rectangle "External Payment Gateway" <<External System>> as PaymentGateway
rectangle "External Shipping Carrier" <<External System>> as ShippingCarrier
rectangle "External Email/SMS Provider" <<External System>> as NotificationProvider

' Group databases
rectangle "Data Stores" {
  database "PostgreSQL DB" <<Database>> as PostgreSQL
  database "MongoDB DB" <<Database>> as MongoDB
  database "Redis Cache/DB" <<Database>> as Redis
  database "Elasticsearch DB" <<Database>> as Elasticsearch
}

' Group Microservices
package "E-commerce Microservices" {
  component "API Gateway Service" <<Microservice>> as APIGateway {
    interface "IClientAPI" as IClientAPI
  }
  component "Auth Service" <<Microservice>> as AuthService {
    interface "IAuthAPI" as IAuthAPI
  }
  component "User Profile Service" <<Microservice>> as UserProfileService {
    interface "IUserProfileAPI" as IUserProfileAPI
  }
  component "Product Catalog Service" <<Microservice>> as ProductCatalogService {
    interface "IProductCatalogAPI" as IProductCatalogAPI
  }
  component "Shopping Cart Service" <<Microservice>> as ShoppingCartService {
    interface "IShoppingCartAPI" as IShoppingCartAPI
  }
  component "Order Service" <<Microservice>> as OrderService {
    interface "IOrderAPI" as IOrderAPI
  }
  component "Payment Service" <<Microservice>> as PaymentService {
    interface "IPaymentAPI" as IPaymentAPI
  }
  component "Shipping Service" <<Microservice>> as ShippingService {
    interface "IShippingAPI" as IShippingAPI
  }
  component "Inventory Service" <<Microservice>> as InventoryService {
    interface "IInventoryAPI" as IInventoryAPI
  }
  component "Review Service" <<Microservice>> as ReviewService {
    interface "IReviewAPI" as IReviewAPI
  }
  component "Notification Service" <<Microservice>> as NotificationService {
    interface "INotificationAPI" as INotificationAPI
  }
  component "Admin Service" <<Microservice>> as AdminService {
    interface "IAdminAPI" as IAdminAPI
  }
}

' Kafka Event Bus
queue "Kafka Event Bus" <<Message Broker>> as Kafka

' Relationships
ClientApp -- IClientAPI
IClientAPI - APIGateway

APIGateway --> IAuthAPI
APIGateway --> IProductCatalogAPI
APIGateway --> IShoppingCartAPI
APIGateway --> IOrderAPI
APIGateway --> IReviewAPI
APIGateway --> IAdminAPI
APIGateway --> IUserProfileAPI

' Inter-service communication (synchronous via REST/HTTP)
OrderService --> IShoppingCartAPI
OrderService --> IUserProfileAPI
OrderService --> IProductCatalogAPI
OrderService --> IInventoryAPI
OrderService --> IPaymentAPI
OrderService --> IShippingAPI
OrderService --> INotificationAPI

ShoppingCartService --> IProductCatalogAPI
ReviewService --> IProductCatalogAPI
AdminService --> IProductCatalogAPI
AdminService --> IOrderAPI
AdminService --> IUserProfileAPI
AdminService --> IAuthAPI

' External API integrations
PaymentService -- PaymentGateway
ShippingService -- ShippingCarrier
NotificationService -- NotificationProvider

' Database integrations
AuthService -- PostgreSQL
UserProfileService -- PostgreSQL
OrderService -- PostgreSQL
PaymentService -- PostgreSQL
ShippingService -- PostgreSQL
InventoryService -- PostgreSQL

ProductCatalogService -- MongoDB
ReviewService -- MongoDB

ShoppingCartService -- Redis

ProductCatalogService -- Elasticsearch

' Asynchronous communication via Kafka
note "Asynchronous communication for event-driven workflows (e.g., order processing, inventory updates, notifications)" as KafkaNote
KafkaNote . Kafka

OrderService ..> Kafka : publishes/consumes events
InventoryService ..> Kafka : publishes/consumes events
NotificationService ..> Kafka : consumes events
PaymentService ..> Kafka : publishes events
ShippingService ..> Kafka : publishes events

@enduml
diagram_versions.deployment diagram:
@startuml
!include <C4/C4_Container>
!include <C4/C4_Deployment>

skinparam defaultFontName Arial
skinparam defaultFontSize 14

title Deployment Diagram for E-commerce Microservices System

cloud "Cloud Platform (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP)" as CloudPlatform {

    node "Kubernetes Cluster" as K8sCluster {

        component "API Gateway Service" <<Docker Container>> as APIGateway
        component "Auth Service" <<Docker Container>> as Auth
        component "User Profile Service" <<Docker Container>> as UserProfile
        component "Product Catalog Service" <<Docker Container>> as ProductCatalog
        component "Shopping Cart Service" <<Docker Container>> as ShoppingCart
        component "Order Service" <<Docker Container>> as Order
        component "Payment Service" <<Docker Container>> as Payment
        component "Shipping Service" <<Docker Container>> as Shipping
        component "Inventory Service" <<Docker Container>> as Inventory
        component "Review Service" <<Docker Container>> as Review
        component "Notification Service" <<Docker Container>> as Notification
        component "Admin Service" <<Docker Container>> as Admin

        APIGateway --> Auth : Authenticates
        APIGateway --> UserProfile
        APIGateway --> ProductCatalog
        APIGateway --> ShoppingCart
        APIGateway --> Order
        APIGateway --> Admin

        Order --> Payment : Initiates payment
        Order --> Shipping : Creates shipment

        Payment --> "Payment Gateway" <<External API>> as PaymentGateway
        Shipping --> "Shipping Carrier API" <<External API>> as ShippingAPI
        Notification --> "Email/SMS Provider" <<External API>> as NotificationProvider

    }

    database "PostgreSQL DB" <<Managed Service>> as PostgreSQL {
        storage "User Profiles"
        storage "Orders"
        storage "Inventory"
        storage "Shipping Addresses"
    }

    database "MongoDB DB" <<Managed Service>> as MongoDB {
        storage "Product Catalog"
        storage "Product Reviews"
    }

    database "Redis Cache" <<Managed Service>> as Redis {
        storage "Session Data"
        storage "Shopping Carts"
        storage "Product Cache"
    }

    database "Elasticsearch" <<Managed Service>> as Elasticsearch {
        storage "Product Search Index"
        storage "Logs/Metrics"
    }

    component "Kafka Cluster" <<Managed Service>> as Kafka

    component "CDN (e.g., CloudFront)" <<Managed Service>> as CDN

}

component "Client Applications" <<Web/Mobile>> as Clients

Clients --> CDN : Serves static assets
Clients --> APIGateway : API Requests

Auth --> PostgreSQL
UserProfile --> PostgreSQL
Order --> PostgreSQL
Inventory --> PostgreSQL
Admin --> PostgreSQL : Manages users, orders, inventory

ProductCatalog --> MongoDB
Review --> MongoDB
Admin --> MongoDB : Manages products, reviews

ShoppingCart --> Redis
APIGateway --> Redis : Caching / Session

ProductCatalog --> Elasticsearch : Indexes products for search

Order --> Kafka : Publishes Order events
Inventory --> Kafka : Consumes Order events
Notification --> Kafka : Consumes Order/Shipping events

@enduml
diagram_versions.sequence diagram:
@startuml
title E-commerce Checkout Process

actor Client as ClientApp
participant "API Gateway Service" as APIGateway
participant "Auth Service" as Auth
participant "Shopping Cart Service" as Cart
participant "Order Service" as Order
participant "Inventory Service" as Inventory
participant "Payment Service" as Payment
participant "Kafka Broker" as Kafka
participant "Notification Service" as Notification
boundary "External Payment Gateway" as ExtPayment

skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center

box "E-commerce System"
    APIGateway
    Auth
    Cart
    Order
    Inventory
    Payment
    Kafka
    Notification
end box

ClientApp -> APIGateway: POST /checkout (cartId, paymentDetails)
activate APIGateway
APIGateway -> Auth: Validate User Token
activate Auth
Auth --> APIGateway: Token Valid
deactivate Auth

APIGateway -> Order: Create Order Request (userId, cartId, paymentDetails)
activate Order
Order -> Cart: Get Cart Items (cartId)
activate Cart
Cart --> Order: Cart Items Details
deactivate Cart

Order -> Inventory: Reserve Items (cartItems)
activate Inventory
Inventory --> Order: Items Reserved (reservationId)
deactivate Inventory

Order -> Payment: Process Payment (userId, amount, paymentDetails)
activate Payment
Payment -> ExtPayment: Authorize & Capture Payment
activate ExtPayment
ExtPayment --> Payment: Payment Success/Failure
deactivate ExtPayment

alt Payment Successful
    Payment --> Order: Payment Confirmed (transactionId)
    Order -> Order: Update Order Status to "Paid"
    Order -> Kafka: Publish OrderPaidEvent (orderId, userId, transactionId, cartItems)
    activate Kafka
    Kafka --> Inventory: Consume OrderPaidEvent (Deduct Stock)
    activate Inventory
    Inventory --> Kafka: Stock Deducted
    deactivate Inventory
    Kafka --> Notification: Consume OrderPaidEvent (Send Order Confirmation)
    activate Notification
    Notification --> Kafka: Confirmation Sent
    deactivate Notification
    Kafka --> Cart: Consume OrderPaidEvent (Clear Cart)
    activate Cart
    Cart --> Kafka: Cart Cleared
    deactivate Cart
    deactivate Kafka
    Order --> APIGateway: Order Created Successfully (orderId)
else Payment Failed
    Payment --> Order: Payment Failed (reason)
    Order -> Order: Update Order Status to "Failed"
    Order -> Inventory: Release Reserved Items (reservationId)
    activate Inventory
    Inventory --> Order: Items Released
    deactivate Inventory
    Order -> Kafka: Publish OrderFailedEvent (orderId, userId, reason)
    activate Kafka
    Kafka --> Notification: Consume OrderFailedEvent (Send Payment Failure Notification)
    activate Notification
    Notification --> Kafka: Notification Sent
    deactivate Notification
    deactivate Kafka
    Order --> APIGateway: Order Creation Failed (reason)
end

deactivate Order
APIGateway --> ClientApp: HTTP 200 OK / 400 Bad Request
deactivate APIGateway

note over Order, Notification: Kafka ensures asynchronous processing, decoupling, and resilience for post-payment actions.
@enduml
diagram_versions.state machine diagram:
@startuml
title Order Status Lifecycle (Order Service)

state "Created" as Created
state "Pending Payment" as PendingPayment
state "Payment Authorized" as PaymentAuthorized
state "Payment Failed" as PaymentFailed
state "Processing" as Processing
state "Shipped" as Shipped
state "Delivered" as Delivered
state "Cancelled" as Cancelled
state "Refunded" as Refunded

[*] --> Created

Created --> PendingPayment : initiatePayment()
Created --> Cancelled : cancelOrder()

PendingPayment --> PaymentAuthorized : paymentSuccess()
PendingPayment --> PaymentFailed : paymentFailure()
PendingPayment --> Cancelled : cancelOrder()

PaymentFailed --> Cancelled : systemCancels()

PaymentAuthorized --> Processing : inventoryReserved()
PaymentAuthorized --> Refunded : cancelOrderWithRefund()
PaymentAuthorized --> Refunded : fraudDetected()

Processing --> Shipped : orderShipped()
Processing --> Refunded : cancelOrderWithRefund()
Processing --> Refunded : outOfStock()

Shipped --> Delivered : deliveryConfirmed()
Shipped --> Refunded : returnInitiated()

Delivered --> Refunded : returnProcessed()

Cancelled : Order terminated without refund.
Refunded : Order terminated with refund.

@enduml
diagram_versions.class diagram:
@startuml
' Styling
skinparam class {
  BorderColor #34495E
  BackgroundColor #ECF0F1
  ArrowColor #34495E
  FontColor #2C3E50
}
skinparam enum {
  BorderColor #34495E
  BackgroundColor #D5DBDB
  ArrowColor #34495E
  FontColor #2C3E50
}
hide empty members

' Entities
class User {
  + id: UUID
  + username: String
  + email: String
  - passwordHash: String
  + createdAt: DateTime
  + updatedAt: DateTime
}

class UserProfile {
  + id: UUID
  + userId: UUID
  + firstName: String
  + lastName: String
  + phoneNumber: String
  + defaultShippingAddressId: UUID
}

class Address {
  + id: UUID
  + street: String
  + city: String
  + state: String
  + zipCode: String
  + country: String
}

class Category {
  + id: UUID
  + name: String
  + description: String
}

class Product {
  + id: UUID
  + name: String
  + description: String
  + price: BigDecimal
  + categoryId: UUID
  + imageUrl: String
  + sku: String
  + createdAt: DateTime
  + updatedAt: DateTime
}

class Inventory {
  + id: UUID
  + productId: UUID
  + quantity: Integer
  + reservedQuantity: Integer
  + lastUpdated: DateTime
}

class Cart {
  + id: UUID
  + userId: UUID
  + createdAt: DateTime
  + updatedAt: DateTime
}

class CartItem {
  + id: UUID
  + cartId: UUID
  + productId: UUID
  + quantity: Integer
  + priceAtTimeOfAddition: BigDecimal
}

class Order {
  + id: UUID
  + userId: UUID
  + orderDate: DateTime
  + status: OrderStatus
  + totalAmount: BigDecimal
  + shippingAddressId: UUID
  + billingAddressId: UUID
  + paymentId: UUID
}

class OrderItem {
  + id: UUID
  + orderId: UUID
  + productId: UUID
  + quantity: Integer
  + priceAtTimeOfOrder: BigDecimal
}

class Payment {
  + id: UUID
  + orderId: UUID
  + amount: BigDecimal
  + currency: String
  + status: PaymentStatus
  + transactionId: String
  + paymentMethod: String
  + paymentDate: DateTime
}

class Shipment {
  + id: UUID
  + orderId: UUID
  + trackingNumber: String
  + carrier: String
  + status: ShipmentStatus
  + shippingDate: DateTime
  + deliveryDate: DateTime
}

class Review {
  + id: UUID
  + productId: UUID
  + userId: UUID
  + rating: Integer (1-5)
  + comment: String
  + reviewDate: DateTime
}

' Enums
enum OrderStatus {
  PENDING
  PROCESSING
  SHIPPED
  DELIVERED
  CANCELLED
}

enum PaymentStatus {
  PENDING
  AUTHORIZED
  CAPTURED
  REFUNDED
  FAILED
}

enum ShipmentStatus {
  PENDING
  SHIPPED
  IN_TRANSIT
  DELIVERED
  EXCEPTION
}

' Relationships
User "1" -- "1" UserProfile : has
UserProfile "1" -- "0..*" Address : manages
User "1" -- "1" Cart : owns
Cart "1" -- "0..*" CartItem : contains
CartItem "1" -- "1" Product : refers to

Category "1" -- "0..*" Product : contains
Product "1" -- "1" Inventory : has stock of

User "1" -- "0..*" Order : places
Order "1" -- "0..*" OrderItem : contains
OrderItem "1" -- "1" Product : refers to

Order "1" -- "1" Payment : processed by
Order "1" -- "1" Shipment : handled by

Order "1" -- "1" Address as ShippingAddress : shipping to >
Order "1" -- "1" Address as BillingAddress : billing to >

User "1" -- "0..*" Review : writes
Product "1" -- "0..*" Review : receives

Order "1" -- "1" OrderStatus : has
Payment "1" -- "1" PaymentStatus : has
Shipment "1" -- "1" ShipmentStatus : has

' Notes for clarity on service ownership
note "Managed by Auth Service & User Profile Service" as UserNote
User .. UserNote

note "Managed by User Profile Service" as UserProfileNote
UserProfile .. UserProfileNote
Address .. UserProfileNote

note "Managed by Product Catalog Service" as ProductCatalogNote
Product .. ProductCatalogNote
Category .. ProductCatalogNote

note "Managed by Inventory Service" as InventoryNote
Inventory .. InventoryNote

note "Managed by Shopping Cart Service" as ShoppingCartNote
Cart .. ShoppingCartNote
CartItem .. ShoppingCartNote

note "Managed by Order Service" as OrderNote
Order .. OrderNote
OrderItem .. OrderNote

note "Managed by Payment Service" as PaymentNote
Payment .. PaymentNote

note "Managed by Shipping Service" as ShippingNote
Shipment .. ShippingNote

note "Managed by Review Service" as ReviewNote
Review .. ReviewNote
@enduml
diagram_versions.package diagram:
@startuml
skinparam packageStyle rectangle
skinparam component {
  BorderColor #34495E
  BackgroundColor #ECF0F1
  ArrowColor #2C3E50
  ArrowFontColor #2C3E50
}
skinparam actor {
  BorderColor #2C3E50
  FontColor #2C3E50
}
skinparam cloud {
  BorderColor #2C3E50
  FontColor #2C3E50
}
skinparam database {
  BorderColor #2C3E50
  FontColor #2C3E50
}

title Microservices E-commerce System - Package Diagram

actor "Client Applications" as Client

cloud "External Payment Gateway" as PaymentGateway
cloud "External Shipping Carrier" as ShippingCarrier
cloud "External Email/SMS Provider" as NotificationProvider

database "Databases" as DB {
  artifact "PostgreSQL"
  artifact "MongoDB"
  artifact "Redis"
  artifact "Elasticsearch"
}

package "User & Identity" {
  component "Auth Service" as AuthService
  component "User Profile Service" as UserProfileService
}

package "Product & Catalog" {
  component "Product Catalog Service" as ProductCatalogService
  component "Inventory Service" as InventoryService
  component "Review Service" as ReviewService
}

package "Order & Checkout" {
  component "Shopping Cart Service" as ShoppingCartService
  component "Order Service" as OrderService
  component "Payment Service" as PaymentService
  component "Shipping Service" as ShippingService
}

package "Gateway & Operations" {
  component "API Gateway Service" as APIGatewayService
  component "Admin Service" as AdminService
  component "Notification Service" as NotificationService
}

Client --> APIGatewayService : Requests
APIGatewayService --> "User & Identity"
APIGatewayService --> "Product & Catalog"
APIGatewayService --> "Order & Checkout"
APIGatewayService --> AdminService : Admin UI Access

"Order & Checkout" --> "User & Identity" : User details
"Order & Checkout" --> "Product & Catalog" : Product/Inventory info
OrderService --> NotificationService : Order updates
PaymentService --> PaymentGateway : Process payments
ShippingService --> ShippingCarrier : Manage shipments

NotificationService --> NotificationProvider : Send notifications

AdminService --> "User & Identity" : Manage users
AdminService --> "Product & Catalog" : Manage products
AdminService --> "Order & Checkout" : Manage orders

"User & Identity" --> DB
"Product & Catalog" --> DB
"Order & Checkout" --> DB
"Gateway & Operations" --> DB : (Admin/Notification config)

@enduml
